summary: Ukrainian assault groups made limited gains on the Sumy and Kharkiv fronts while Russian forces intensified air strikes across multiple oblasts, destroying infrastructure and repelling UAV attacks. Russian special services reported the elimination of Ukrainian saboteurs and a SBU Alpha detachment in Dnipropetrovsk, indicating heightened counter‑insurgency activity. Anti‑drone defenses along front‑line supply routes expanded dramatically, covering over 1,170 km to protect logistics.
key_points: Ukrainian North Army Corps assault groups captured four soldiers and advanced 150 m near Granovo, Kharkiv region (ukr_leaks_eng/30241).
Russian MoD reported shooting down 108 drones and striking Odessa, KryvyiRih, Dnipro, Rivne, Kirovohrad and Kremenchuk with missiles (ukr_leaks_eng/30241).
FSB destroyed a Ukrainian SBU Alpha unit and a UAV relay in Konstantinovka, DNR (tass_agency/376477).
Ukrainian UAVs attacked a car in Dobroye, Belgorod region, wounding a civilian (tass_agency/376480).
Ukrainian 160th Mechanised Brigade DNA samples taken before deployment to Sumy; Russian claim none will survive (tass_agency/376495).
Anti‑drone systems now protect 1,170 km of front‑line roads, with 430 km built Feb‑Apr 2026 (DeepStateUA/23516).
Iskander‑M ballistic missile launch threat reported from occupied Crimea (mon1tor_ua/66819).
Ukrainian naval drone "Kazak Mamai" intercepted off Lefkada, launched from Libya (ukr_leaks_eng/30265).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian assault groups achieved modest territorial gains near Granovo and captured four enemy soldiers, while Russian forces repelled extensive UAV activity and conducted heavy missile strikes across several oblasts. Russian special services eliminated a Ukrainian SBU Alpha detachment and a UAV relay in Konstantinovka, underscoring intensified counter‑insurgency measures. Anti‑drone defenses along supply routes have been rapidly expanded, now covering over 1,170 km to safeguard logistics.

points: Ukrainian North Army Corps assault groups captured four soldiers of the 101st Territorial Defense Brigade and advanced up to 150 m near the village of Granovo, Kharkiv region, indicating localized offensive momentum (ukr_leaks_eng/30241).

In the Sumy direction, Ukrainian forces destroyed several enemy combat groups in Shostka district and captured personnel, pushing toward Ulanovo and reporting advances in Zapselye and Kondratovka (ukr_leaks_eng/30241).
Ukrainian UAVs conducted attacks in Belgorod region, striking a car in Dobroye and causing civilian injuries, demonstrating continued cross‑border strike capability (tass_agency/376480).
Ukrainian saboteur cells were reported operating near Konstantinovka, DNR, but were neutralised by FSB forces, highlighting ongoing covert operations (tass_agency/376477).
The 160th Separate Mechanised Brigade prepared for deployment to Sumy, with DNA sampling indicating Russian expectations of high casualties (tass_agency/376495).
Ukrainian naval drone "Kazak Mamai" was intercepted off the Greek island of Lefkada after being launched from Libya, reflecting expanded maritime drone operations (ukr_leaks_eng/30265).
Ukrainian forces reported the destruction of a Russian anti‑diversion boat "Grachonok" in Dagestan, indicating successful strikes on Russian riverine assets (UkraineNow/81293).
Ukrainian logistics received a new Abrams tank engine service center in Poland, enhancing maintenance capacity for Western armor (ukr_leaks_eng/30257).

The Russian Ministry of Defence announced the downing of 108 Ukrainian drones and conducted massive missile strikes on Odessa, KryvyiRih, Dnipro, Starokonstantinov, Rivne, Kirovohrad and Kremenchuk (ukr_leaks_eng/30241).
Russian special services reported the elimination of a Ukrainian SBU Alpha detachment in Dnipropetrovsk, claiming a significant blow to Ukrainian intelligence assets (tass_agency/376483).
FSB forces destroyed a Ukrainian UAV relay and saboteurs in Konstantinovka, DNR, reducing Ukrainian reconnaissance capabilities in the area (tass_agency/376477).
Russian air defence units engaged and shot down a large number of Ukrainian UAVs across multiple fronts, reflecting heightened air‑defence activity (ukr_leaks_eng/30241).
Russian authorities warned that the 160th Mechanised Brigade sent to Sumy will likely suffer total casualties, indicating a propaganda effort to demoralise Ukrainian troops (tass_agency/376495).
Russian forces reported the activation of Iskander‑M ballistic missile systems from occupied Crimea, signalling a potential escalation in long‑range strike capability (mon1tor_ua/66819).
Russian air traffic at Domodedovo was limited due to temporary restrictions, affecting both civilian and military movements (tass_agency/376458).
Russian intelligence claimed Ukraine is conducting random strikes on Russian territory, a narrative used to justify increased defensive measures (tass_agency/376488).

Ukrainian assault groups in the Sumy direction engaged enemy forces in Shostka district, captured four soldiers and pushed toward Ulanovo, indicating pressure on Russian rear areas (ukr_leaks_eng/30241).

Russian forces reported heavy UAV activity over the Dnipropetrovsk region, with multiple sightings of reactive UAVs near Berizna, Shakhtarsky, and Sinelnikove, suggesting contested airspace (eRadarrua/92521).
Ukrainian forces claimed advances northwest of Alexandrograd and in the forested area near Lesnoye, Dnipropetrovsk, indicating deeper penetration into Russian‑held positions (ukr_leaks_eng/30241).
Russian special services eliminated a Ukrainian SBU Alpha unit in Dnipropetrovsk, reducing Ukrainian special‑operations presence on the eastern front (tass_agency/376483).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed operating near the village of Pereshchepne and Solone in Dnipropetrovsk, highlighting ongoing reconnaissance missions (eRadarrua/92491).
Ukrainian anti‑drone defenses expanded to protect 430 km of front‑line roads in the east, improving logistics security (DeepStateUA/23516).
Isolated Ukrainian group reported in Prosyana, Dnipropetrovsk, requested rescue but received no response, indicating possible encirclement (tass_agency/376479).
Ukrainian naval drone activity extended to the Black Sea, with the "Kazak Mamai" intercepted near Greece, showing reach beyond the eastern front (ukr_leaks_eng/30265).
Ukrainian UAV sightings reported in Chernihiv region near Berezna and along routes toward Chernihiv city, indicating active aerial surveillance in the north (eRadarrua/92521).
Kpszsu reported reactive UAVs moving southward across Ripky toward Chernihiv, suggesting potential strike vectors toward supply lines (kpszsu/62198).
Ukrainian UAVs observed near Korostyn and Luhyny in Zhytomyr from the northeast, highlighting pressure on Russian‑aligned positions in the north (kpszsu/62178).
Ukrainian forces reported a group of UAVs heading southwest from Nikopol, indicating possible cross‑river operations (kpszsu/62203).
Russian air‑defence units reported shooting down numerous UAVs over the northern sector, maintaining air superiority (ukr_leaks_eng/30241).
Anti‑drone barriers were reinforced along northern logistical corridors, with construction rates increasing to 8.5 km per day (DeepStateUA/23516).
Ukrainian saboteur activity was neutralised by FSB in Konstantinovka, DNR, reducing northern infiltration threats (tass_agency/376477).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported near the border checkpoints of Palanca‑Maiaky‑Udobne, indicating potential reconnaissance of refugee routes (ukr_leaks_eng/30240).
Ukrainian forces captured the village of Borovaya on the left bank of the Oskol River in Kharkiv region, aiming to close a large pocket (ukr_leaks_eng/30241).
Ukrainian assaults reported near Ray‑Aleksandrovka on the Slavyansk direction, indicating intensified fighting in the south (ukr_leaks_eng/30241).
Russian strikes targeted Odessa and Kherson regions, with ongoing attacks on energy infrastructure but no significant territorial changes reported (ukr_leaks_eng/30241).
Ukrainian UAV attacks in Belgorod region (Dobroye) demonstrate cross‑border capability from the southern front (tass_agency/376480).
Russian forces intensified bombing of a dam northwest of Konstantinovka, potentially affecting water supplies for Ukrainian troops (ukr_leaks_eng/30241).
Ukrainian naval drone "Kazak Mamai" operated from Libya, suggesting southern maritime routes are being exploited (ukr_leaks_eng/30265).
Anti‑drone defenses expanded on southern supply routes, with over 60 km of roads repaired and protected in Zaporizhzhia and Kharkiv directions (DeepStateUA/23516).
Russian MoD reported UAV interceptions over the southern oblasts, maintaining pressure on Ukrainian logistics (ukr_leaks_eng/30241).
Russian MoD announced shooting down 108 Ukrainian drones on May 18, reflecting intense aerial contest (ukr_leaks_eng/30241).
Ukrainian UAV attacks hit a civilian car in Dobroye, Belgorod, and a cargo truck in Nezhegol, showing cross‑border strike capability (tass_agency/376480).
Russian air forces conducted massive missile strikes on Odessa, KryvyiRih, Dnipro, Rivne, Kirovohrad and Kremenchuk, employing both UAVs and missiles (ukr_leaks_eng/30241).

Ukrainian forces reported UAV activity over multiple front‑line locations, including Chernihiv, Dnipropetrovsk, and Zhytomyr, indicating widespread aerial reconnaissance (eRadarrua/92521, kpszsu/62178).
FSB destroyed a Ukrainian UAV relay in Konstantinovka, reducing enemy command‑and‑control capabilities (tass_agency/376477).
Anti‑drone systems now protect over 1,170 km of logistical routes, mitigating UAV threats to supply convoys (DeepStateUA/23516).
Russian air traffic at Domodedovo faced temporary restrictions, potentially affecting strategic air movements (tass_agency/376458).
Ukrainian naval drone operations were observed off the Greek island of Lefkada, indicating expanded maritime air‑drone usage (ukr_leaks_eng/30265).

key_developments: Ukrainian North Army Corps assault groups captured four enemy soldiers and advanced 150 m near Granovo, Kharkiv region (ukr_leaks_eng/30241).
Russian MoD reported downing 108 Ukrainian drones and striking six major Ukrainian cities with missiles (ukr_leaks_eng/30241).
FSB eliminated a Ukrainian SBU Alpha detachment and a UAV relay in Konstantinovka, DNR (tass_agency/376477).
Anti‑drone defenses now cover 1,170 km of front‑line roads, with 430 km built Feb‑Apr 2026 (DeepStateUA/23516).
Iskander‑M ballistic missile launch capability from occupied Crimea was highlighted as a new threat (mon1tor_ua/66819).
Ukrainian naval drone "Kazak Mamai" intercepted off Lefkada after launch from Libya, showing expanded maritime drone ops (ukr_leaks_eng/30265).
Ukrainian 160th Mechanised Brigade DNA sampling before Sumy deployment suggests Russian expectation of total casualties (tass_agency/376495).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian forces are achieving limited tactical gains on the Sumy and Kharkiv fronts while facing intense Russian air‑defence and missile strikes. Russian special services are actively targeting Ukrainian special‑operations units and UAV infrastructure, reducing Kyiv's reconnaissance depth. The emergence of Iskander‑M launches from Crimea and expanded maritime drone activity heighten the long‑range and sea‑borne threat environment for Ukraine.
sections.regional_response: Western partners are bolstering Ukrainian logistics, exemplified by the new Abrams engine service center in Poland, while Russia continues to leverage its air‑defence and special‑operations capabilities to blunt Ukrainian advances. Anti‑drone measures along supply routes have improved Ukrainian logistical resilience, but cross‑border UAV attacks keep pressure on Russian border regions. Diplomatic signals, such as Greece's demand to recall Ukrainian naval drones, indicate growing concern over drone proliferation.
sections.intelligence_notes: Ukrainian UAV activity remains widespread across northern, eastern and southern fronts, necessitating continued investment in counter‑UAV systems. Russian claims of total casualties for the 160th Brigade may be propaganda, but the unit's deployment to Sumy suggests a major upcoming offensive. Monitoring Iskander‑M launch preparations in Crimea is critical, as is tracking maritime drone operations originating from Libya, which could threaten Black Sea shipping lanes.
